Japanese: １０月２０日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 20, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、荷星　三郎の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側も、 準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 今日は、審理の最終日になります。
English: Today will be the final day of this trial.

Japanese: 検察側、弁護側とも、決定的な 証拠の提出をするように。
English: I hope both the prosecution and the defense will be able to present decisive evidence.

Japanese: では、御剣検事。 ‥‥冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well, Mr. Edgeworth. Your opening statement.

Japanese: 昨日の法廷で、弁護人によって 新しい説が提示された。
English: In yesterday's session, the defense presented us with a new theory for this case.

Japanese: 殺人現場は、実は 第２スタジオだった、という。
English: He claims that the scene of the crime was, in fact, Studio Two.

Japanese: 本日は、実際にコテージにいた 人間を証人として呼び、
English: Today, I will call on people present in the Studio Two trailer that day.

Japanese: その事実関係をハッキリさせたい。
English: From their testimonies, the truth will become clear.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥わかりました。
English: Hmm... very well!

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥今日はちょっと、 ひかえめだな‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth seems a bit on edge today...)

Japanese: では、 証人を呼んでいただきましょう。
English: You may call your first witness, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 事件当日、第２スタジオの コテージで会議をしていた、
English: The prosecution calls Ms. Dee Vasquez to the stand.

Japanese: 姫神　サクラプロデューサーを。
English: Ms. Dee Vasquez is a producer who was present in the Studio Two trailer that day.

Japanese: 証人。 ‥‥名前と、職業を。
English: Will the witness state her name and occupation?

Japanese: 姫神　サクラ。 英都撮影所のプロデューサー。
English: Dee Vasquez. I am a producer for Global Studios.

Japanese: 証人は、事件当日、 第２スタジオのコテージで‥‥
English: On the day of the murder, you were in the trailer in Studio Two...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことは、みんな 知ってるでしょ。
English: As everyone here knows, yes?

Japanese: よけいなことはキライ。
English: I dislike needless banter.

Japanese: どうしてもやりたいなら、 アタクシぬきでやりなさい。
English: If you must pontificate, do it when I'm not here.

Japanese: むぐぐ。
English: Mrrph!

Japanese: で、では、証人。
English: V-very well, Ms. Vasquez.

Japanese: 事件当日のことを、 《証言》してください。
English: Please give the court your testimony concerning the day of the murder.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ このヒトがハンニンなんだよね！
English: Nick! I know she did it! Make her pay!

Japanese: ああ。‥‥そうさ！
English: R-right!

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、シッポを つかんでやるぞ‥‥）
English: (If she's guilty, I'll catch her with her pants down... So to speak.)

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- The Day of the Murder --

Japanese: ‥‥正午の少し前、第２スタジオの コテージに入ったわ。
English: I entered the trailer, oh, a little before noon.

Japanese: 会議が始まったのは、正午。 終わったのは４時。
English: The meeting began at 12:00 sharp. It ended at 4:00.

Japanese: それから、リハーサルがあるから 第１スタジオへ。
English: There was to be a rehearsal afterwards, so we went to Studio One...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥そう、つかれてたから、 宇在に運ばせたわ。
English: I was fatigued, so I had Sal take me.

Japanese: 会議の休憩は、 ２時３０分から１５分間だった。
English: At 2:30, we took a 15-minute break in the meeting.

Japanese: 宇在といっしょに、コテージの前で ホネつきステーキを食べていたわ。
English: Sal and I ate t-bone steaks on the table in front of the trailer.

Japanese: 第１スタジオに行って、そこで イブクロの死体を見つけたわけ。
English: We found Hammer's body later, when we all went to Studio One.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなところね。
English: That's all.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証言で、１つだけ 気になったことがあります。
English: I have a question about one part of your testimony.

Japanese: “つかれていたから、宇在に 運ばせた”‥‥というのは？
English: You were "fatigued" so you had Sal "take" you...?

Japanese: クルマ。
English: The van.
Hmm?

Japanese: ライトバンがあったでしょ？ 第２スタジオ。
English: There's a van at Studio Two.

Japanese: あれ、宇在に運転させたの。
English: I had him take me in that.

Japanese: ‥‥サルマゲどんのクビも あって、あぶなかったし。
English: I thought it might be risky to walk, what with that monkey's head toppling over.

Japanese: 《ライトバン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Van added to the Court Record.

Japanese: な、なるほど‥‥。
English: I... I see!

Japanese: では、弁護人。 《尋問》‥‥を。
English: Very well. Mr. Wright, your cross-examination.

Japanese: 最後の勝負だよ。 ‥‥がんばって！
English: This is the final battle, Nick! Let's do it!

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- The Day of the Murder --

Japanese: ‥正午の少し前に、第２スタジオの コテージに入ったわ。
English: I entered the trailer, oh, a little before noon.

Japanese: 会議が始まったのは、正午。 終わったのは４時。
English: The meeting began at 12:00 sharp. It ended at 4:00.

Japanese: それから、リハーサルがあるから 第１スタジオへ。
English: There was to be a rehearsal afterwards, so we went to Studio One.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥そう、つかれてたから、 宇在に運ばせたわ。
English: I was fatigued, so I had Sal take me.

Japanese: 会議の休憩は、 ２時３０分から１５分間だった。
English: At 2:30, we took a 15-minute break in the meeting.

Japanese: 宇在といっしょに、コテージの前で ホネつきステーキを食べていたわ。
English: Sal and I ate t-bone steaks on the table in front of the trailer.

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥どう？
English: Well, Nick? Find anything?

Japanese: もう少し、たくさんしゃべって くれるとありがたいんだけど‥‥。
English: I kind of wish she'd said a little more...

Japanese: 相手はヤリ手プロデューサー だもんね‥‥。
English: It's hard. I think she's pretty used to being the boss.

Japanese: でも、きっとどこかに ムジュンがあるはずだよ！
English: But I'm sure you'll find some kind of contradiction in what she said... somewhere!

Japanese: お１人で？
English: By yourself?

Japanese: そう。
English: Yes.

Japanese: スタッフエリアには カオを出さなかったんですか。
English: And you didn't stop in at the Employee Area?

Japanese: そう。
English: No.

Japanese: 会話、はずみませんね。
English: Are you always this... terse?

Japanese: そう？
English: Yes.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh dear...)

Japanese: そのあいだ、席を立った人は いませんか？
English: Did no one leave during the meeting?

Japanese: いないわ。
English: No one.

Japanese: も、もっとしゃべりましょうよ。
English: C-come on... let's talk a bit more about that.

Japanese: 証人は、おしゃべりをしに来た ワケではない！
English: The witness did not come here to engage in idle conversation!

Japanese: 証言のジャマをしないでもらおう！
English: Stop interrupting the testimony.

Japanese: アナタもよ、検事さん。
English: The same could be said to you, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ムヤミに机をたたかないで。 ‥‥耳ざわりだから。
English: And please stop banging on your little desk. It hurts my ears.

Japanese: ‥‥うぐ。
English: Mmph!

Japanese: それは、何時ごろですか？
English: And when exactly was that?

Japanese: わすれたわ。
English: I've forgotten.

Japanese: お、思い出そうという気は ないんですか。
English: Maybe you could try remembering?

Japanese: ないわ。
English: No.

Japanese: 会議が４時に終わり、リハーサルは ５時からだった。
English: The meeting ended at 4:00, and the rehearsal was scheduled for 5:00...

Japanese: おそらく、そのあいだだろう。
English: I would think it was between those times.

Japanese: （“おそらく”も何も、 そんなのアタリマエだろうが！）
English: (You "would think"!? C'mon, all you did was state the obvious, Edgeworth!)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold on!

Japanese: “宇在に運ばせた”‥‥って、 どういう意味ですか？
English: You had Sal "take" you? What does that mean!?

Japanese: ‥‥さっき、言ったわ。
English: It means what I just said.

Japanese: ライトバン、運転させたの。 ‥‥宇在に。
English: I had Sal take me in the Studio Two van.

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば、 そんなこと、言ってたっけ‥‥）
English: (Oh... oh, right.)

Japanese: その時間は、何を‥‥？
English: And what were you doing during that time...?

Japanese: 机をたたかないで。
English: Don't hit your desk.

Japanese: ‥‥うるさいわ。
English: It irritates me.

Japanese: そうだぞ！　弁護人‥‥！ あ。
English: Yeah! Mr. Wright...!
Oops.

Japanese: 休憩した時間は‥‥１５分。
English: It was a 15 minute break...

Japanese: それを証明してくれる人は いますか！
English: Do you have someone who can verify this?

Japanese: ‥‥宇在。
English: Sal.

Japanese: い、いや。 ‥‥その他の人たちは？
English: N-no... I mean someone else?

Japanese: じゅうぶんでしょ。 １人いれば。
English: One person is enough.

Japanese: （休憩時間のあいだに 事件が起こったとすれば‥‥）
English: (If she committed murder during that break...)

Japanese: （当然、ステーキなど 食べているヒマはない‥‥）
English: (She wouldn't have had time to eat a t-bone steak!)

Japanese: （“ステーキを食べていなかった” という証拠はないかな‥‥？）
English: (If I had some evidence that proved she didn't eat that steak...)

Japanese: 思ったとおり、そう来ましたね。
English: Hah! As I thought!

Japanese: “ホネつきステーキを 食べていた”‥‥。
English: You claim you ate a t-bone steak!

Japanese: しかし、それはおかしいんです！
English: But I say you did not!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: W-what's this, now, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Look at this.

Japanese: 皿‥‥ですか。
English: It's... a plate?

Japanese: これは、スタッフエリアの テーブルにあった皿です。
English: This plate was on the table in the employee room.

Japanese: ごらんのように、 大きなホネが残っている。
English: As you can see, a large bone has been left behind.

Japanese: 当然だろう。 ‥‥ホネつきステーキなんだから。
English: Mr. Wright. Need I remind you it was a T-BONE STEAK!?

Japanese: そう。‥‥当然です。
English: Exactly my point!

Japanese: しかし。 ‥‥思い出してください。
English: Remember, if you will...

Japanese: コテージの外にあった、 テーブルの上を‥‥。
English: Ms. Vasquez and Mr. Manella ate at a table outside the trailer.

Japanese: このテーブルの皿の上には‥‥
English: Yet there were no bones left on the plates!

Japanese: ホネは残っていない！
English: The plates were bare!

Japanese: 姫神さん！
English: Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: もし、ホネつきステーキを 食べたのなら、
English: Tell me, how can a person eat a t-bone steak...

Japanese: 当然ですが、ホネが残ります。
English: and not leave the bone?

Japanese: つまり、あなたは‥‥
English: I think I know how!

Japanese: ホネもいっしょに食べたんです！
English: You ate the bone, too!

Japanese: どうしてそうなるの、 なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Um, Nick... are you sure?

Japanese: さすがのあたしも、ホネは 残すよ！
English: I mean, I love steak, but even I don't eat the bone!

Japanese: ぼくだって残すよ！
English: Hey, I don't eat it either!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥私も、ホネは残しますよ。
English: I, too, leave the bone.

Japanese: も、もう一度、おねがいします。
English: Um, can I try that again?

Japanese: あなたが食べたステーキには、 ホネがなかったのです！
English: Elementary! You were eating a boneless steak!

Japanese: だとしたら、
English: Mr. Wright, say you are right...

Japanese: そんな、指をつきつけて 言うほどのこともありませんね。
English: What would that prove?

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Um, right, Your Honor.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 姫神さんの証言を思い出して！
English: Remember her testimony?

Japanese: “宇在といっしょに、 ホネつきステーキを食べていたわ”
English: She said she ate a "t-bone steak"!

Japanese: ハッキリ“ホネつき”って 言ってたじゃない！
English: She said "bone"!

Japanese: そのとおり。 ‥‥真宵ちゃんをためしたのさ。
English: Correct! I was... testing you!

Japanese: ウソだぁ。
English: Yeah, sure, Nick.

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥もう一度おねがいします。
English: Sorry... Can I try that one more time?

Japanese: あなたは休憩時間中、 ステーキは食べなかったのです！
English: You didn't eat any steak during that break!

Japanese: きっとステーキは、焼却炉にでも 捨ててしまったのでしょう！
English: You took your steak and threw it somewhere... like that incinerator!

Japanese: で、では‥‥、
English: I... I see!

Japanese: 姫神さんは休憩中、 何をしていたと‥‥？
English: Then what was Ms. Vasquez doing during her break?

Japanese: 決まっているじゃないですか！
English: Isn't it obvious, Your Honor?

Japanese: 宇在カントクを いじめていたんですよ！
English: She was picking on Mr. Manella!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 たまにあなたは、
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ホンキだかジョウダンだか わからないことを言いますね。
English: Sometimes this court finds it hard to tell whether you are joking or being serious.

Japanese: ええ。
できれば、 ホンキだけにしてください。
English: I would prefer it if you remained serious!

Japanese: はあ。 そうします‥‥、できれば。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor. I'll do my best.

Japanese: もちろん、トノサマンに 会っていたんですよ！
English: She was meeting with the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥！
English: A-are you saying...!?

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: ステーキを食べるため、 コテージを出た姫神さんは‥‥
English: As Ms. Vasquez left the trailer to eat her steak...

Japanese: トノサマンに出くわしたのです！
English: She ran into the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: そしてあなたは、彼を‥‥
English: And then you did it.

Japanese: 殺害したのです。 ‥‥その手でッ！
English: You killed him with your own hands!

Japanese: べ‥‥弁護人！
English: M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: そ、その発言は、問題が‥‥
English: I-isn't that a little presumptuous!?

Japanese: どういうことだ！ 成歩堂、キミは‥‥
English: What kind of stunt are you trying to pull, Wright!?

Japanese: かまわないわ。
English: Let him claim what he wants....

Japanese: アタクシがやった、と？
English: You say I did it?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥おもしろいわ。
English: Fascinating!

Japanese: ひさしぶりに、タイクツを わすれられそうね‥‥。
English: And here I was worrying that today would be as boring as all the rest.

Japanese: いいわ。
English: Very well.

Japanese: これから、アナタとアタクシの チエくらべと行きましょう。
English: Let us have a battle of wits, you and I.

Japanese: （受けて立つぞ‥‥！）
English: (Any day, Vasquez!)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥しっかりね！
English: Good luck, Nick!

Japanese: そうね‥‥。
English: Let's see...

Japanese: たしか、凶器は トノサマン・スピアーだったわね。
English: What was that murder weapon again? Oh yes, the "Samurai Spear."

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes...?

Japanese: アタクシ、見たとおり かよわい女よ。
English: I am, as you can see, a woman of petite stature.

Japanese: こんなアタクシが、重いスピアーを 自由にあつかえると思って？
English: How could I possibly use that heavy spear as a weapon?

Japanese: 不可能ではないでしょう！
English: It's not impossible!

Japanese: 不可能ではないかもしれない。 しかし、問題はそんなコトではなく
English: Well, maybe it's not impossible, but--

Japanese: おだまりなさい。
English: Silence.

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシが話してるの。
English: I'm speaking.

Japanese: ぐむむ。
English: Mmmph!

Japanese: 被害者は、胸を正面から 刺されているわ。
English: The victim was stabbed through the chest from the front, yes?

Japanese: つまり、被害者は犯人に 気づいていた、ということでしょ。
English: I believe this would indicate that the victim was aware of the killer's presence.

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみは、 軽く、動きやすくできてるわ。
English: Now, the Steel Samurai costume is quite light and mobile.

Japanese: 腕力も、走る速さも、すべて 負けているアタクシが、
English: Compared to the victim, I am weaker and slower...

Japanese: 被害者とマトモに戦って、 勝てるわけがない。
English: I couldn't possibly win such a fight!

Japanese: しかも、写真が示しているとおり、
English: Not to mention, there is proof I did not have the spear!

Japanese: そのスピアーは、 被害者自身が持ってきたものよ。
English: The photograph of the Steel Samurai shows that the victim himself was holding it!

Japanese: アタクシが、彼からそれを チカラずくで奪ったとでも言うの？
English: Or do you mean to suggest that I could have wrested it away from him?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだぞ！　成歩堂！
English: Y-yeah! Take that, Wright!

Japanese: それでも“やった”と言うなら、 その証拠を見せたまえッ！
English: If you still think she did it, show us proof!

Japanese: う‥‥うううん‥‥。
English: Umm... er...

Japanese: ねえ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーのデータを ちょっと見てみて。
English: Look at the data we have on that Samurai Spear in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥気になること、ない‥‥？
English: Isn't there something in there that gets to you...?

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーのデータ？
English: Something odd about the Samurai Spear...?

Japanese: あっ！
English: Wait!

Japanese: どうしましたか？　弁護人？
English: Something the matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: さっきの発言を取り消します。
English: I'd like to take back my previous claim.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたありませんね。
English: Very well, I won't hold you to your claim.

Japanese: しかし、ペナルティは受けて もらいます。
English: But I'm afraid I will have to penalize you.

Japanese: （ひゃあ‥‥）
“発言を取り消す”‥‥？ どういうことだ。
English: What's this? You're taking back your claim?

Japanese: とにかく、話をもどしましょう。
English: Let's backtrack for a moment.

Japanese: “証人がトノサマン・スピアーを 使うことができたかどうか？”
English: I want to consider whether the witness could have used the Samurai Spear or not!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、あなたでなくても、 あれを武器にして戦うのは、
English: I think you would have a great deal of difficulty using that as a weapon.

Japanese: フツーなら、ムリでしょうね。
English: Pretty much anyone would.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hah.

Japanese: わかってるじゃない。
English: You see?

Japanese: でも。
English: Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、わかりません。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: “わからない”だと！ ‥‥そんなイイカゲンなこと‥‥
English: You "don't know"!? What kind of a lawyer are you!?

Japanese: アタクシが話してるの。 ‥‥ジャマしないで。
English: Quiet. I'm speaking.

Japanese: むむむ。
English: M-m-mmph!

Japanese: “わからない”ですって？ そんなイイカゲンなこと‥‥
English: You "don't know"!? What kind of a lawyer...

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーで、あなたが 戦えたかどうか‥‥。
English: I don't know whether you could have fought with the Samurai Spear...

Japanese: そんなことは、このさい 関係ないんですよ‥‥。
English: However, that has no bearing on this case!

Japanese: どういうこと？
English: Meaning?

Japanese: 凶器は、トノサマン・スピアーでは なかったんですからね。
English: The Samurai Spear was not the murder weapon!

Japanese: なッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: な、何を言い出すのですか！
English: What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: スピアーは、死体のそばに ころがっていたのですぞ！
English: The spear was found lying next to the victim's body!

Japanese: 証拠をお見せしましょう。
English: I have proof, Your Honor.

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーが 凶器ではないという証拠を‥‥！
English: I can prove that the Samurai Spear was not used as the murder weapon!

Japanese: 違いますね、それは。
English: Wrong, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、何も言ってませんよ。
English: But I haven't said anything yet...?

Japanese: 慣れてきましたからね。 あやしいフンイキを感じます。
English: I know you well enough by now to know when you're going to present the wrong evidence.

Japanese: あたしも感じる。 ‥‥綾里家の霊力で。
English: I can sense it too, with my Fey blood...

Japanese: （ようするに、この証拠品は 違う、ということだな）
English: (Okay, okay, so I picked the wrong evidence! Enough with all the sensing!)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥本当の証拠をお見せします。
English: Right. Okay, here's the real evidence, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証拠は、言うまでもなく こいつですよ。
English: I present my proof: the so-called "murder weapon"!

Japanese: 凶器‥‥そのものだとッ！
English: B-but, that IS the murder weapon!

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Not so fast.

Japanese: 午前中の打ち合わせで、 このスピアーは一度、折れている！
English: Remember that this spear broke during the morning action scene run-through?

Japanese: 折れたスピアーを修理したのは ‥‥オバチャンです。
English: But, someone fixed the spear.
It was the security lady!

Japanese: ガムテープでね。
English: She fixed it with duct tape!

Japanese: そんなもので、
English: Now tell me, how is it possible...

Japanese: ぶあつい着ぐるみをつけた被害者の 胸を、刺せるわけがない！
English: for someone to stab a man who is wearing a thick costume with this!? Through the chest!

Japanese: せ、静粛に‥‥！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: 弁護人！　どういうことだ！
English: Mr. Wright! What are you driving at!?

Japanese: 自分が何を言ってるか‥‥
English: Think about what you're--

Japanese: おだまりッ！
English: Silence!

Japanese: アタクシをムシしないこと。
English: I'm the one testifying here and I will be heard!

Japanese: うむむ‥‥。
English: Mmmph...!

Japanese: どういうことかしら？
English: Are you quite sane?

Japanese: 自分が何を言ってるか、 わかってるの？
English: Are you even aware of what you're saying?

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーが 凶器じゃないというなら、
English: If the Samurai Spear was not the murder weapon, then pray tell what was!?

Japanese: いったい、衣袋は どうやって殺されたと言うの？
English: How was Hammer killed?

Japanese: どうですか？　イブクロさんを 殺害した凶器を、提示できますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Can you tell us what weapon was used to kill Mr. Hammer?

Japanese: もちろんできません。
English: Of course I can't.

Japanese: も、“もちろん”‥‥？
English: "O-of course"!?

Japanese: なんだそのタイドは！
English: Can the antics, Wright!

Japanese: わからないにしても、 もう少し言い方があるでしょう！
English: I have to agree, Mr. Wright. At least pretend that you think you might know!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥思ったことを そのまま言ってしまった‥‥）
English: (Urk. Note to self: think before speaking.)

Japanese: （オトナの世界は、 気をつかうよなあ‥‥）
English: (Tough crowd...)

Japanese: できなければ、こんなコトは 言いません。
English: Would I make a claim like that if I couldn't tell you what the weapon was?

Japanese: いつもながら、ウサンくさいほどの 自信ですね‥‥。
English: I'm afraid your confidence can sometimes be unfounded, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: では、示していただきましょう。 イブクロさんを殺害した凶器を。
English: Very well, let's see this murder weapon, then.

Japanese: おねがいッ！　なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおして！
English: Think about this some more!

Japanese: え？　なんで？
English: Huh? Think?

Japanese: だって‥‥。
English: Please!

Japanese: そんなものが凶器なワケ、 ないじゃない‥‥。
English: How could that possibly have been the murder weapon!?

Japanese: （それもそうか‥‥）
English: (Hmm. She has a point.)

Japanese: どうしましたか？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: いえっ。べつに。 ‥‥なんでもありませんよ。
English: N-no, Your Honor. Nothing!

Japanese: ‥‥この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this photo.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、これは‥‥。
English: W-what is this?

Japanese: ‥‥階段の上にいるのは‥‥ い、衣袋　武志ですか‥‥！
English: Why, that's Jack Hammer standing at the top of the stairs!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: さわぎがおさまらなければ、 審理を中止しますぞ！
English: If I cannot have order this trial will be suspended!

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: どういうことですか これは！
English: What is this all about!?

Japanese: ‥‥今から５年前。
English: This photograph is from five years ago.

Japanese: 英都撮影所で、 不幸な事故がありました。
English: There was an unfortunate accident at Global Studios.

Japanese: 見てのとおりの事故が、ね。
English: This is a photo of that very accident.

Japanese: このことは、いっさい 外部には知らされなかった。
English: Not a word of this was leaked to the outside.

Japanese: 英都だけのヒミツだったのです。
English: It was a close-kept secret at the studios.

Japanese: それが、今回の事件と なんの関係があるというのだ！
English: What does this have to do with the current case!?

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥。
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、わかりませんか？
English: You still can't see it?

Japanese: 階段から転落して、鉄柵で 胸を刺された、この男を見て‥‥。
English: See the fallen man in this photo? See how the fencepost pierced him through the chest?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、ああッ！
English: Wh... what!?

Japanese: な‥‥なんということだ‥‥。
English: Are you saying...!?

Japanese: この事故があったのは、今から ５年前でした。
English: Yes.

Japanese: それと同じコトが‥‥
English: What happened five years ago...

Japanese: ふたたび、起こったのです！
English: has happened again!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ ‥‥つづけなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! Continue!

Japanese: 事件のあった日の、 午後２時３０分！
English: It's 2:30 PM on the day of the murder!

Japanese: 姫神　サクラは、コテージのそとで イブクロさんと会ったのです！
English: Ms. Dee Vasquez meets Jack Hammer outside the Studio Two trailer!

Japanese: そして彼女は、
English: Then she did it!

Japanese: 故意か事故かはわかりませんが、 イブクロさんを‥‥
English: She pushed Mr. Hammer off the stairs onto the fence!

Japanese: つきおとしたのです！ ‥‥鉄柵の上に。
English: Just like Mr. Hammer had himself done five years ago!

Japanese: ５年前、イブクロさん自身が そうしたように‥‥。
English: Though whether she did it on purpose or by accident, I cannot say.

Japanese: つまり、被害者は‥‥ 衣袋　武志は‥‥、
English: I-in other words... th-the victim, Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: 自分が人を死なせてしまった、 そのやり方で、
English: He died in the same way that he caused another man to die...

Japanese: 今度は、自らのイノチを 落とした、というワケかッ！
English: five years ago!

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことになりますね。
English: Precisely.
Ironic, isn't it?

Japanese: フ‥‥フッ。 よくできた話。
English: Hoh hoh. Very creative, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: その、出まかせの才能‥‥。 ウチの脚本家としてほしいわね。
English: I could use a man like you on my script writing staff.

Japanese: 何か、反論がありますか？
English: You deny that what I say is true?

Japanese: ‥‥いい？
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: アナタの言うとおり、衣袋が コテージで死んだとしましょう。
English: Let's say, for the sake of argument, that Hammer died at the trailer as you say.

Japanese: でも実際は、死体は 第１スタジオで見つかったのよ。
English: Yet, the body was found at Studio One, was it not?

Japanese: アクダイカーンの着ぐるみの 中からね。
English: And in the Evil Magistrate's costume, no less.

Japanese: 死体を第１スタジオに運んで、 着ぐるみの中に入れる‥‥、
English: Are you then claiming that I carried the body to Studio One...

Japanese: そんなの、休憩時間の１５分じゃ ゼッタイに不可能よ。
English: And returned to the trailer, all in the space of a 15 minute break?

Japanese: いったい私は、どうやって 死体を始末できたと言うの？
English: How could I have disposed of the body!?

Japanese: コテージでは、２時３０分から １５分だけ、休憩をとった。
English: The break in the meeting at the trailer lasted 15 minutes, from 2:30 to 2:45.

Japanese: その間に、衣袋　武志を階段から つきおとして殺害して、
English: Could Jack Hammer have been pushed off the stairs to his death...

Japanese: 第１スタジオに運んで、 着ぐるみの中に入れる‥‥。
English: then carried to Studio One and placed inside his costume?

Japanese: たしかに、時間的にムリが あるな‥‥。
English: There wasn't enough time!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに。
English: Hmm... indeed.

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 ‥‥死体の問題については？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? How could she have dealt with the body?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥時間的に、 １５分じゃムリですね‥‥。
English: 15 minutes is kind of a short amount of time...

Japanese: そうでしょ。
English: You see?

Japanese: ムリなことは、できない。
English: Even I cannot do the impossible.

Japanese: だから、アタクシは‥‥
English: Therefore, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 犯人じゃない。 ‥‥よろしいかしら？
English: I didn't do it. Agreed?

Japanese: よろしくありません。
English: Not agreed!

Japanese: や‥‥やれば できるんじゃないですか？
English: You'd be surprised! I bet she could do it!

Japanese: ニンゲンて、死んだ気になれば、 思ったよりも、やるもんですよ。
English: When people think their life depends on it, they can do amazing things!

Japanese: ここでニンゲンを語られても 困ります。
English: Mr. Wright, this is not an episode of "That's Incredible."

Japanese: “１５分ですべてのことができた” というならば‥‥
English: If you claim she did everything in 15 minutes...

Japanese: （“その証拠を出せ”ってか？）
English: (Let me guess: "Show Me Proof!")

Japanese: そのとおり！ ‥‥証拠を出してもらおう！
English: Correct! Show me proof!

Japanese: （そんなの、ムリに 決まってるだろう‥‥）
English: (How am I supposed to do that...?)

Japanese: ならば、テキトーなことは 言わないでもらいたい！
English: Then I won't have you making unsubstantiated claims!

Japanese: （なんでさっきから、ぼくの 考えていることがわかるんだ？）
English: (Wait a second, how does he know what I'm thinking!?)

Japanese: ハッ‥‥！
English: Hah!

Japanese: その冷や汗の感じで、なんとなく わかるのだよ。
English: I need only look at the cold sweat running down your face to know what you're thinking!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥もう一度、 考えなおしてください。
English: Mr. Wright. Perhaps you'd like to reconsider your claim?

Japanese: 死体を運び出す方法が‥‥ 他にもあったら、どうでしょうか。
English: What if she had another way to carry the body, other than with her own hands?

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: For instance?

Japanese: 何も、ムリにすべてを １５分におさめることはない。
English: Actually, for that matter, there was no need for her to do the deed in 15 minutes!

Japanese: あなたには、死体を運び出す 方法が、１つだけあったのです。
English: And there was a way for you to carry that body.

Japanese: い‥‥いいわ。 聞かせてもらいましょう？
English: I-interesting! Let's hear it then.

Japanese: では、弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 示してもらいましょう。 ‥‥死体を運んだ方法を。
English: Please show us how she carried the body.

Japanese: 姫神　サクラは、これを使って 死体を現場に運んだのです！
English: Dee Vasquez used this to carry the body to Studio One!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: はい。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: そんなもので、 死体を運べますか？
English: Mr. Wright, how can you carry a dead body with that?
Umm...

Japanese: もう一度、考えてもらいましょう。
English: Please rethink your statement.

Japanese: 姫神さん。
English: Ms. Vasquez.

Japanese: あなたは、死体を第１スタジオに 運んだのです。
English: You carried the body to Studio One.

Japanese: この、ライトバンに乗せて。
English: And you used the studio van to do it!

Japanese: あなたはさっき、 こう証言しましたね‥‥。
English: Recall your testimony...

Japanese: 会議のあと、リハーサルがあるから 第１スタジオへ。
English: There was to be a rehearsal afterwards, so we went to Studio One.

Japanese: つかれてたから、宇在に 運ばせたわ。
English: I was fatigued, so I had Sal take me.

Japanese: ライトバンがあったでしょ？ ‥‥宇在に運転させたの。
English: There was a van there, right? I had Sal drive me.

Japanese: あなたは、ライトバンに死体を 乗せて第１スタジオへ行ったんだ！
English: You used the van to carry the body to Studio One!

Japanese: そして、みんなが集まる前に、 死体を着ぐるみに入れたんです！
English: Then, before everyone else got there, you put the body into the Magistrate costume!

Japanese: ちょっと待て！
English: Hold on, Wright!

Japanese: ライトバンを運転したのは、 宇在カントクだったはずだ！
English: Don't forget, it was Sal Manella that drove the van!

Japanese: 弁護人は、宇在カントクが 共犯だったと言うのか！
English: Are you suggesting that Mr. Manella was a conspirator?

Japanese: もちろん違います！
English: Of course he wasn't!

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Hah!

Japanese: 宇在の目を盗んで死体を ライトバンに乗せて、
English: So, without Mr. Manella noticing anything, she put the body in the van...

Japanese: 宇在の目を盗んで着ぐるみに 入れた‥‥
English: Then managed to put the body into the costume...?

Japanese: ホンキで そう主張するつもりかッ！
English: Are you seriously suggesting that is possible!?

Japanese: ホンキなワケ、 ないじゃないですか。
English: Of course I'm not serious.

Japanese: は‥‥はぅぅッ！
English: Ha... hah!?

Japanese: ジョークですよ。 ‥‥この場をなごませる。
English: I was kidding. To, er, lighten up the proceedings.
Li..light...

Japanese: なごむかッ！
English: Lighten!? Y-you can't lighten up a murder!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 主張するときは、つねに ホンキでおねがいします。
English: The court requests you refrain from making claims in jest.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: トーゼン、宇在カントクは 共犯です！
English: Of course, Sal Manella has to have been a conspirator!

Japanese: 死体をライトバンに積み込むのも、 着ぐるみに入れるのも、
English: The body had to be placed in the van, and put into the costume.

Japanese: 姫神さん１人では、 ムリですからね！
English: There's no way Ms. Vasquez could have done that alone!

Japanese: それに、トノサマンの着ぐるみも 始末させたんじゃないですか？
English: Also, don't forget that they had to dispose of the Steel Samurai costume.

Japanese: イブクロさんの血が ついていますからね！
English: They had to... because it was covered in Mr. Hammer's blood!

Japanese: おそらく、あの小さな焼却炉で 焼いたのでしょう‥‥。
English: They probably burned it in that small incinerator.

Japanese: どうですか！　姫神さん！
English: Well, Ms. Vasquez!

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、つづけますか？
English: Shall I continue?

Japanese: もう、ケッコウ。
English: No need.

Japanese: アナタ‥‥見かけ以上に やるわね。
English: You're smarter than you look, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: フッ‥‥。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: アタクシの負け。 ‥‥楽しかったわ。
English: I lose. You win. It... was fun.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: それで？
English: So... what happens next?

Japanese: “？”じゃありませんよ。
English: Don't "?" me!

Japanese: 何か、言うことはないんですか？
English: Don't you have anything to say!?

Japanese: 何を言えばいいの？
English: What would you like me to say?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “負けましたわ”とかなんとか。
English: "I lost"? Something like that!

Japanese: それならさっき、言ったわ。
English: I just said that.

Japanese: つ、つまり！
English: W-wait, so you mean...

Japanese: 姫神　サクラさん！ ‥‥あなたですね？
English: Dee Vasquez! So, it was you?

Japanese: 衣袋　武志を殺害したのは！
English: You killed Jack Hammer!

Japanese: わからないわ。
English: Who can say?

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: なんでそうなるのかしら？
English: Are you sure I did?

Japanese: いい？
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: アタクシ、あなたとチエくらべを したのよ。
English: We just engaged in a battle of wits.

Japanese: その結果、たしかにアタクシ、 犯行は可能だったみたい。
English: And the result of that battle? You proved the possibility that I murdered Hammer.

Japanese: そのとおりです！
English: R-right!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、それはあくまで 可能性の話。
English: But... that is only a possibility.

Japanese: 証拠となれば、またベツよ。
English: Proof is another thing altogether.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠、ないじゃない。
English: You lack decisive proof, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥な、な、な、な、な、な、な、 な、な、な、な、な、な、な‥‥
English: Wha... wha... wha... wha...

Japanese: なんですってェェェェェェェェェ！
English: WHAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！
English: Order!

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ どう思われますか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, your thoughts?

Japanese: う‥‥ううむ‥‥、むむむ。
English: Umm... uhh... mmmph.

Japanese: も、もちろん、証人の 言うとおりである‥‥！
English: O-of course, it's as the witness says!

Japanese: たしかに、きわめてアヤシイが、 証拠となると‥‥‥ない‥‥。
English: Certainly, it does seem very likely she did it... but, uh, there's no proof!

Japanese: （御剣のヤツが‥‥迷っている！）
English: (Edgeworth isn't sure!)

Japanese: どうしたのかしら？
English: Well?

Japanese: アタクシは今日、 証人としてここに来たのよ。
English: I came here as a witness today.

Japanese: 質問がなければ、もう帰るわ。
English: If you've no more questions, I'll be leaving.

Japanese: （く‥‥くそっ！ ‥‥どうする‥‥！）
English: (Urk! What do I do now!?)

Japanese: 姫神さん！ ‥‥あなたがやったんだ！
English: Ms. Vasquez! You did it!

Japanese: それは質問じゃないわ。
English: That wasn't a question.

Japanese: あなたがやったんですか？
English: Um, did you do it?

Japanese: いいえ。
English: No.

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.
Y-yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: どうも、その質問には、 意味がないようですね‥‥。
English: I'm not sure that question helped this court get any closer to a verdict.

Japanese: も、もう一度、《証言》を おねがいします！
English: I... I'd like you to testify again!

Japanese: もう一度、ですか‥‥？
English: Testify... again?

Japanese: あきらめの悪い男‥‥。
English: Know when to give up, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: いいかしら。
English: Think about it.

Japanese: アタクシ、もう一度証言 することになっても、
English: Even if I were to testify again, what's the point?

Japanese: さっきとまったく同じ ことしか言わないわよ。
English: I can only say what I said before. The truth, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: それこそ、ヒトコトももらさず、 カンペキに同じことをね。
English: I've already said all there is to say.

Japanese: そうしたらアナタ、どうするの？
English: What would happen if I said it again?

Japanese: うむむむ‥‥
English: Uh...

Japanese: 同じ質問をして、同じ答えを 聞いて‥‥。
English: You'd ask the same questions, get the same answers.

Japanese: 時間のムダよ。
English: A waste of time.

Japanese: どうしましたか？　弁護人。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥机を叩いているあいだに 質問を考えようと思いましたが、
English: I was hoping I'd come up with a question while pounding on my desk.

Japanese: 何も浮かびませんでした。
English: I didn't.

Japanese: それは、ごくろうさまでした。
English: You have my sympathies.
(Damn... damn!)

Japanese: （今度こそ、これまでか‥‥）
English: (To come so far, only to fail...)

Japanese: では、質問もないようなので‥‥、
English: As it seems there are no further questions...

Japanese: これで姫神さんに対する 質問を終了します！
English: I would like to end the cross-examination of the witness, Ms. Vasquez.

Japanese: どうしました？　御剣検事。
English: Yes, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥イギをとなえるあいだに 何か質問を考えようと思ったが、
English: I was hoping to come up with a question while I was objecting, Your Honor...

Japanese: 思い浮かばなかった。
English: I didn't.

Japanese: そうですか。 ‥‥では、今度こそ
English: I see... Very well.

Japanese: お待ちいただこうッ！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 証人！　もう一度、《証言》を！
English: I request that the witness testify again!

Japanese: アナタ、たしか検事でしょ？
English: You are the prosecutor, are you not?

Japanese: なんでそんなにしつこく アタクシを‥‥？
English: Why are you badgering me? I'm your witness!

Japanese: とにかく、もう一度だけ、 証人の話を聞きたい！
English: I... I just want to hear your testimony again.

Japanese: どういうことなの？ 裁判長。
English: Does this make any sense to you, Your Honor?

Japanese: さっきと同じこと、 またくり返すのかしら？
English: I don't see that we have anything to gain by repeating the last several minutes.

Japanese: 御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 同じことを証言してもらう わけには行きません。
English: I, too, see little point in making Ms. Vasquez repeat herself.

Japanese: いったい、何を 証言してもらうのですか？
English: What exactly did you want her to testify about?

Japanese: う‥‥うむぅ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Mmmph! Well, yes, um...

Japanese: そ、そうだな。 ええと‥‥そのぅ‥‥。
English: Indeed! Verily, I say... Ergo!

Japanese: 死体を発見してからあとの ことを、お聞きしたい！
English: I want to hear about what happened... after they found the body!

Japanese: 死体を見つけてから、あと‥‥？
English: After they found the body...?

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥《証言》をおねがいします。
English: The witness will testify to the court concerning this matter.

Japanese: ‥‥ふぅ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: どうして、御剣検事が‥‥？
English: Why did Edgeworth...?

Japanese: ‥‥さあね。
English: Who knows?

Japanese: あいつにも、わかったんだろ？ ‥‥誰が犯人か。
English: He probably realized she did it, too.

Japanese: でも、でも！
English: But... but!

Japanese: “ヒコクニンをみんなユーザイに するのが、オレのるーるだ”って、
English: Wasn't he the one that said he always gets a guilty verdict...?

Japanese: エラそうに言ってたのに‥‥。
English: After all that...

Japanese: （ミツルギ‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ～死体を見つけたあとのこと～
English: -- After Finding the Body --

Japanese: 死体を見つけたときは、 宇在や警備員の大場がいたわ。
English: I was with Sal and Oldbag, the security lady, when we found the body.

Japanese: スタッフの子もいたし。 いなかったのは、荷星だけ。
English: The assistant was there, too. Only Powers was absent.

Japanese: すぐに警察を呼んだわ。 荷星もおくれてやってきて。
English: I immediately called the police. Then Powers showed up.

Japanese: 警備員の大場が大さわぎだった。 “こいつが犯人だ”って。
English: The security lady, Oldbag, was quite agitated. Pointing at Powers, saying "he did it!"

Japanese: アタクシは、その場にいなかった ことにしてもらって、
English: I asked to be left out of the proceedings.

Japanese: いったんコテージに、台本や 演出ノートを取りにもどったわ。
English: I went back to the trailer to get my script and direction notes.

Japanese: そしてその日は、 そのまま帰ったわね。
English: Then I went home.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: この証言に、とくに問題が あるとは思えませんが‥‥。
English: I see no issues raised by this testimony...

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Mr. Wright, your cross-examination.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ あとひと押しだよ！
English: Okay, Nick, we're close, real close!

Japanese: あの人、強がっては いるけど、
English: She may be acting tough now...

Japanese: なるほどくん、もう 追いつめているんだから。
English: But if you put her on the spot she's sure to falter!

Japanese: 最後のカギを見つけたら、 きっと勝てるよ！
English: Find the key you need and twist the truth out of her, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥うん！
English: Right!

Japanese: ～死体を見つけたあとのこと～
English: -- After Finding the Body --

Japanese: 死体を見つけたときは、 宇在や警備員の大場がいたわ。
English: I was with Sal and Oldbag, the security lady, when we found the body.

Japanese: スタッフの子もいたし。 いなかったのは、荷星だけ。
English: The assistant was there, too. Only Powers was absent.

Japanese: すぐに警察を呼んだわ。 荷星もおくれてやってきて。
English: I immediately called the police. Then Powers showed up.

Japanese: 警備員の大場が大さわぎだった。 “こいつが犯人だ”って。
English: The security lady, Oldbag, was quite agitated. Pointing at Powers, saying "he did it!"

Japanese: アタクシは、その場にいなかった ことにしてもらって、
English: I asked to be left out of the proceedings.

Japanese: いったんコテージに、台本や 演出ノートを取りにもどったわ。
English: I went back to the trailer to get my script and direction notes.

Japanese: そしてその日は、 そのまま帰ったわね。
English: Then I went home.

Japanese: ケガした衣袋にアクションはムリ だから、持って行かなかったの。
English: I knew that Hammer was injured and couldn't do any action scenes, so I left them behind.

Japanese: ここで“何か”を 見つけられなかったら、
English: You have to find something to peg this on her here...

Japanese: 今までのことがみんな、 ムダになっちゃうんだよ。
English: Or everything we've done till now will have been wasted!

Japanese: なるほどくんを信じてる！ だから、なるほどくんも‥‥。
English: I believe in you, Nick! Go get 'em!

Japanese: それは、何時ごろの ことでしたか？
English: What time was that?

Japanese: リハーサルの集合時間は ５時だったから、
English: We were to meet at 5:00 for the rehearsal.

Japanese: そのころだったと思うけど。
English: So I'd say it was then.

Japanese: あなたと宇在カントクは、 何時にスタジオへ？
English: When exactly did you and Mr. Manella arrive at the studio?

Japanese: その１０分ぐらい前かしら？
English: About 10 minutes before.

Japanese: 死体を着ぐるみに詰めたり なんかは、しなかったわよ。
English: I wasn't stuffing any bodies into costumes, mind you.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥）
姫神さんがスタジオについたとき、 他のスタッフのみなさんは？
English: Where were the other employees when you got to the studio?

Japanese: まだ、いなかったわ。
English: They weren't there yet.

Japanese: それならば！
English: Well!?

Japanese: 宇在カントクに手伝わせて、 死体を着ぐるみに‥‥
English: Then you could have put Hammer in that costume, with Sal Manella's help!

Japanese: お待ちなさいな。
English: Oh?

Japanese: だから、さっき言ったでしょ。
English: What did I just say?

Japanese: “死体を着ぐるみに詰めたり なんかは、しなかった”って。
English: Did I not just say "I wasn't stuffing any bodies into any costumes"...?

Japanese: ヒトの話は、よく聞くものよ、 ボク。
English: You should try to listen to what people are saying, boy.

Japanese: （ボ‥‥ボク）
English: (B-boy!?)

Japanese: 証人。 ‥‥つづけてください。
English: The witness will continue her testimony.

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'd better leave this one be.)

Japanese: （こっちの手など、きっと お見とおしだろうからな‥‥）
English: (I'm sure she'd see through anything I had to throw at her, anyway.)

Japanese: 荷星さん‥‥ ヒルネしてたんですよね？
English: Mr. Powers was taking a nap, correct?

Japanese: 本人はそう言ってるわね。
English: So he says.

Japanese: 誰も、呼びに 行かなかったんですか？
English: Did no one go to call him?

Japanese: ‥‥死体。
English: Remember...

Japanese: は？
English: Remember... what?

Japanese: 死体を前にして、そんな 冷静でいられるわけないでしょ。
English: There was a dead body lying in front of us. Yet you expect me to be calm?

Japanese: （“殺人犯だ”って言われても そんなに冷静なクセに‥‥）
English: (You certainly seemed calm enough when I accused you of murder!)

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (*sigh*)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ ‥‥それは逆じゃないでしょうか。
English: Wait a second! Wouldn't that be the other way around!?
The other way around?

Japanese: 殺人が起こって、１人だけ いない人間がいたんですよ。
English: You discovered a murdered body, and one person was missing, right?

Japanese: 探しませんか？　ふつう。
English: Wouldn't you normally go looking for the missing person!?
Hmm...

Japanese: そのとおりですね。
English: I have to agree with the defense on this one.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？　証人？
English: Well, Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: フン。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: そりゃ、リクツはそうかもね。
English: Logically speaking, perhaps.

Japanese: でも、実際にアタクシたち、 それどころじゃなかったの。
English: But no one there was thinking very logically, I assure you.

Japanese: それに。
English: Also.

Japanese: 荷星は、トノサマンの着ぐるみを 着ないと、イマイチ目立たなくて。
English: Without his Steel Samurai costume, Powers doesn't really stand out.

Japanese: よくロケバスに置いていかれたり していたわ。
English: We often left him on the bus when shooting at location. No one noticed he was missing.

Japanese: （カワイソウな荷星さん‥‥）
English: (Poor Powers...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 それじゃあ、しょうがないですね。
English: Hmm... Well, I suppose that explains that.

Japanese: では、証人。‥‥つづきを。
English: Very well, Ms. Vasquez. You may continue.

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'll hold off.)

Japanese: （こっちの手など、きっと お見とおしだろうからな‥‥）
English: (She'd just blow off anything I had to say, anyway.)

Japanese: そのあいだ、みなさんは 何を‥‥？
English: What was everyone else doing at that time?

Japanese: 誰も、ヒトコトもクチを きかなかったわ。
English: No one said a thing.

Japanese: 重い、重いチンモク‥‥。
English: It was silent. Completely silent.

Japanese: ‥‥そこに、やっと起きた 荷星がやってきたわ。
English: So we stood there until Powers finally arrived on the scene.

Japanese: “オクレちゃった！ ゆるしてオクレ！”
English: "Sorry! Sorry I'm late!"

Japanese: ‥‥って言いながら。 その場がさらに凍りついたわね。
English: Then... silence. Dead silence.

Japanese: ‥‥こんな感じにね。
English: Silent.

Japanese: ね、ね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey... Hey, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてかな？ けっこう、おもしろいのに。
English: Why's everyone so quiet?

Japanese: 重いフンイキも なごみそうなもんだけどね。
English: Someone should do something to lighten things up!

Japanese: （オオバ‥‥って、あの オバチャンだよな‥‥）
English: (Oldbag... the security lady.)

Japanese: オバチャンは、どうして 荷星さんを‥‥？
English: Why did the security lady think Powers had done it?

Japanese: 彼がスタジオに向かうのを 見たから、と言ってた。
English: She said because she saw him going toward the studio.

Japanese: （‥‥ああ、トノサマンの ことか‥‥）
English: (Right... the Steel Samurai.)

Japanese: 刑事は、オバチャンの証言だけで 荷星さんを逮捕したんですか？
English: Did the detective arrest Mr. Powers based solely on the security lady's word?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。
English: Perhaps not.

Japanese: あとで聞いた話では、
English: She seems to have also given the detective that photograph...

Japanese: 彼女、写真を刑事に わたしたみたい‥‥。
English: Of course, I heard all this after the fact.

Japanese: （例の、ゲートのカメラが撮った 写真だな‥‥）
English: (That photograph... meaning the one taken by the security camera.)

Japanese: まあ彼女、衣袋のことになると、 見さかいがなくなるから。
English: She's not known for exhibiting the best sense of judgment when it comes to Hammer.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥とくに手がかりに なりそうなことはないなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm. I don't think there's anything in there I can use.)

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'll hold back, I think.)

Japanese: （こっちの手など、きっと お見とおしだろうからな‥‥）
English: (I'm sure she's already anticipated anything I could throw at her.)

Japanese: “いなかったことに”‥‥？
English: You "asked to be left out"?

Japanese: そう。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 他の人は、取り調べを 受けたみたいだけど。
English: It seems that everyone else was questioned.

Japanese: アタクシ、ああいうの、 キライだから。
English: And I'm really not fond of that sort of thing.

Japanese: なんで、そんな自分勝手な ことを‥‥？
English: But, you can't just decide who the police will and won't question!

Japanese: 英都撮影所の人間は、 思いやりがあってね。
English: Thankfully, the studio thinks otherwise.

Japanese: 会議をしていたアタクシたちが 犯人のハズはないからって、
English: They were nice enough to understand that, since we were in a meeting...

Japanese: かばってくれたの。
English: There was no way we could have been involved. Thus, they covered for us.

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (Covered for you... or covered it up for you?)

Japanese: （会議に出ていたのは、 エラいさんに、スポンサー‥‥）
English: (There must have been a lot riding on it, with all those bigwigs at the meeting...)

Japanese: （どうせ、例の“プロ”でも 使ったんだろう‥‥）
English: (I'm sure her "professionals" helped convince everyone they didn't require questioning.)

Japanese: 台本や演出ノート‥‥？
English: The script and your direction notes?

Japanese: 大事なものよ。 ‥‥盗まれちゃまずいわ。
English: Yes, they're quite valuable. It wouldn't do to have them stolen.

Japanese: まあ、それはそうですね。
English: No, I guess not.

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'll hold back, I think.)

Japanese: （こっちの手など、きっと お見とおしだろうからな‥‥）
English: (I'm sure she's already anticipated anything I could throw at her.)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってくださいよ。
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: そもそも第１スタジオには、 リハーサルのために来たはずです。
English: I thought you came to Studio One for a rehearsal!

Japanese: なんで台本やノートを 持っていなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you bring your script and notes!?

Japanese: リハーサルなんか、できないって 思っていたから。
English: Well, I was under the impression that we wouldn't be able to rehearse anything.

Japanese: どうしてです？
English: Why?

Japanese: ‥‥殺人があったでしょ、 なんにしても。
English: There was a murder, after all!

Japanese: リハーサルなんか できるわけがないわ。
English: Who could think of rehearsing after that!

Japanese: （まあ、そりゃそうか‥‥）
English: (Oh, yeah, I guess...)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では、証人、 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well. Ms. Vasquez, please continue...

Japanese: 証人ッ！
English: Your Honor!!!

Japanese: 今の発言には、問題がある！
English: I have an objection to the witness's last statement!

Japanese: よろしいか。
English: Think!

Japanese: “リハーサルがないと思ったから 台本を持っていかなかった”‥‥
English: Ms. Vasquez said she didn't bring the script because there wouldn't be a rehearsal.

Japanese: それではまるで‥‥、
English: Don't you see what that means?

Japanese: コテージを出る前から、すでに 死体があると知っていたようだ！
English: She would have had to know about the murder before going to Studio One!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 御剣検事！
English: Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 今の発言ですが‥‥、
English: What you have just said...

Japanese: たしかに、指摘のとおりです。
English: is a reasonable observation.

Japanese: しかし‥‥。検察側の人間の とる行動とは思えません。
English: However, I find it hard to understand why the prosecution would make such a move!

Japanese: まるで‥‥そう、弁護人では ないですか！
English: Or are you thinking of a career change to defense!?

Japanese: ご心配いただくのは ありがたいが‥‥
English: I... appreciate the concern, Your Honor.

Japanese: 私のとるべき行動を 決めるのは、私だ。
English: I will stand by my statement, however, regardless of how the court sees my role here.

Japanese: さあ、証人。 ‥‥答えていただきたいッ！
English: Now, Ms. Vasquez...
Do you have an explanation?

Japanese: （‥‥ミツルギに‥‥ 助けられた‥‥？）
English: (I can't believe Edgeworth is helping me!)

Japanese: フン‥‥。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 検事は弁護士とグル、ってわけ？
English: So the prosecution is in cahoots with the defense? What kind of court is this?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいわ。
English: No matter.

Japanese: アタクシ、そういう意味で 言ったのではなくてよ。
English: I think you misunderstood me.

Japanese: リハーサルがないと思ったのは、 それなりの理由があるわ。
English: I had a perfectly good reason to believe there would be no rehearsal.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥では、証言を修正して もらいましょう。
English: Very well, the witness will change her testimony to reflect this reason.

Japanese: 取り調べは受けなかった わけですか？
English: So you weren't questioned?

Japanese: そうね。
English: That's correct.

Japanese: アタクシ、そういうの、 キライだから。
English: I find these things... distasteful.

Japanese: この裁判のはじめのころ、 あなた方、コテージ組は、
English: Thinking back to when we first began this trial...

Japanese: 撮影所には、いなかったことに なっていましたよね‥‥？
English: We were under the impression that there was no one in the trailer at all.

Japanese: そうね。
English: I suppose.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: だって、アタクシたち、 ずっと会議をしてたのよ。
English: Well, we were in a meeting the whole afternoon.

Japanese: わざわざ、警察に話すのも メンドウじゃない。
English: What's the point of talking about that to the police?

Japanese: （‥‥ツミのイシキみたいな ものは、まったくないみたいだ）
English: (She has got to be the least guilt-ridden guilty person I've ever met...)

Japanese: （やめておこう‥‥）
English: (I'll hold back for now.)

Japanese: （こっちの手など、きっと
English: (I'm sure she's anticipated anything I had to say...)

Japanese: お見とおしだろうからな‥‥）
どうしてケガのこと、 知っているんですか？
English: How did you know about the injury?

Japanese: ど、どうして、って。
English: H-how did I know!?

Japanese: ‥‥宇在よ。
English: Sal.

Japanese: アイツから聞いたわ。
English: He told me.

Japanese: （宇在カントクか‥‥）
English: (Sal the director... Hmm...)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと、なるほどくん！
今の証言‥‥よく聞いて！ なんか、ヘンだよ。
（‥‥何か、見落としたか‥‥？）
“ケガをした、イブクロさん”‥‥
English: You "knew that Hammer was injured"...?

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Don't you think that's a little funny?

Japanese: ケガをしたのは、 トノサマンの方‥‥
English: I mean, it was the Steel Samurai who was injured!

Japanese: 荷星　三郎なんですよ！
English: It was Will Powers!

Japanese: な‥‥、なんだって！
English: Wh... what!?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ ‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Mr. Wright! Explain yourself!

Japanese: たしかに、イブクロさんは 足を引きずっていた！
English: Yes, Mr. Hammer is dragging his leg in this photo.

Japanese: しかし、それはあくまで、荷星さん のフリをするためだった！
English: But that's because he was pretending to be Mr. Powers!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうこと？
English: P-pretending...?

Japanese: 午前中の打ち合わせで、 たしかにケガ人が出ました。
English: One person was injured in the run-through that morning.

Japanese: しかし、それは荷星さんであって、 イブクロさんではない！
English: That person was Mr. Powers, not Mr. Hammer!

Japanese: なぜあなたは、ケガをしたのが イブクロさんだと思ったんですか！
English: Now, why did you think it was Mr. Hammer who was injured?

Japanese: う‥‥宇在。 宇在に聞いたんだよ！
English: I-I already said it was Sal who told me!

Japanese: そ、そうだ！ アイツがカン違いしたのさ！
English: Th-that's right! He must have gotten it wrong.

Japanese: それはあり得ません。
English: I think not.

Japanese: 宇在カントクは、打ち合わせに 参加していました。
English: Mr. Manella was at the run-through that morning.

Japanese: だから、荷星さんの足のケガの ことは、よく知っています！
English: He would have known it was Mr. Powers who was injured. He saw it happen!

Japanese: 彼が間違えるはずはない！
English: Why would he tell you the wrong person?

Japanese: 姫神さん！　‥‥あなたは、
English: Ms. Vasquez!

Japanese: 聞き間違いを したんじゃないですか？
English: You must have heard Mr. Manella wrong!

Japanese: 宇在カントクは“荷星さんがケガを した”と言ったのに、
English: Even though he said Mr. Powers was injured...

Japanese: あなたはそれを“イブクロさん”と カン違いしたのです！
English: You thought he was talking about Mr. Hammer!

Japanese: 言われてみれば‥‥。
English: Now that you mention it...

Japanese: そんな気もするわね。
English: Yes, I believe you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、 そうだったんでしょう。
English: I'm sure that's what happened.

Japanese: そうですとも。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Nick!

Japanese: それじゃあ、あのヒトを 追い詰められないよ！
English: You're supposed to be putting her on the spot, not helping her out!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに。 話題がなくなっちまったよ。
English: Oh, right... I kind of talked myself out of that one, didn't I?

Japanese: 早く！　やりなおして！
English: Quick! Start over!

Japanese: あなたは、実際に‥‥、
English: Actually, you didn't hear anything from Mr. Manella.

Japanese: 足を引きずっている イブクロさんを見たんです！
English: You saw Mr. Hammer limping with your own eyes!

Japanese: しかし‥‥。
English: Yes, but...

Japanese: ケガをしたのは、 荷星　三郎のはずですが‥‥？
English: Wasn't it Mr. Powers who was injured...?

Japanese: ここで、 思い出してほしいんです。
English: Yes, but remember!

Japanese: イブクロさんも、足を 引きずっていたじゃないですか。
English: Mr. Hammer was dragging his leg, too!

Japanese: 荷星さんのフリをするために！
English: He was pretending to be Mr. Powers!

Japanese: あっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: それは、いつだったか‥‥？
English: And when was he doing this...?
Well...

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみを着て 第２スタジオへ向かい‥‥、
English: It would have been after he put on the Steel Samurai costume...

Japanese: そして、 殺害されるまでのあいだだ‥‥。
English: and went to Studio Two. Where he was murdered!

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 姫神さん！
English: Ms. Vasquez!

Japanese: あなたは、足をひきずった トノサマンと会ったのです！
English: You met him, didn't you? You saw the Steel Samurai limping!

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみには、 イブクロさんが入っていた！
English: And Mr. Hammer was in that Steel Samurai suit!

Japanese: だからこそ、あなたは‥‥
English: That's why you were confused!

Japanese: “イブクロさんがケガをした” と、カン違いしたのです！
English: That's why you thought it was Mr. Hammer who had been injured that morning!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! I will have order!

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: 何か反論は？
English: Can you refute this claim?
Hrrmmmm... hmph!

Japanese: （今度こそ、反論なんか できるわけがないさ！）
English: (Yeah, let's see you sneak out of this one, Vasquez!)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥
English: Very well!

Japanese: じゃあ、 聞かせてもらおうじゃないの！
English: I have a question for you!

Japanese: （真犯人というものは、 どうしてこうシツコイんだ‥‥）
English: (Why are the real killers always so... persistent?)

Japanese: アタクシが衣袋の死を望む、 その理由はなんなのさ？
English: Why in the world would I want Mr. Hammer dead?

Japanese: 落ちぶれていたとはいえ、 スターだったんだよ。
English: Yes, he'd fallen on hard times, but he was a star!

Japanese: アイツが死んだって、アタクシには なんのトクもないじゃないか！
English: I had nothing to gain from his death! Nothing!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: “動機”‥‥というヤツですな。
English: Yes, you would need a motive.

Japanese: たしかに。彼女がなぜ 被害者を殺害したのか‥‥？
English: Why would she have killed the victim?

Japanese: その理由は、まだアキラカに されていませんな‥‥。
English: If there is a reason, it is unclear to this court.

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。
English: Does the defense have anything to say on the matter?

Japanese: ズバリ、動機を証明する ことはできますか？
English: In other words, can you prove she had a motive?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick, no!

Japanese: ここで“できない”って 言っちゃったら‥‥
English: Saying you can't prove it now would be admitting defeat!

Japanese: あのヒト、逃げきっちゃうよ！
English: She'll get away!

Japanese: でも‥‥ムリだよ！ そんな証拠は‥‥。
English: But... but what proof could I possibly have!?

Japanese: ムリでもなんでも、 ここは勝負しかないって！
English: I don't care if it's impossible, you still have to fight this one!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。
English: Ugh...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Okay.

Japanese: 証拠品をお見せしましょう。
English: I have proof, and I'll show it to you!

Japanese: この事件の“動機”を 物語る証拠品は‥‥
English: I present to the court evidence as to Ms. Vasquez's motive in this murder.

Japanese: これだぁ！‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: Psst, Maya, look. This is it.

Japanese: な、なんであたしに見せるの？
English: Why... are you showing that to me, Nick?
And why are you whispering?

Japanese: ちょっと、実験。
English: I was just testing it out on you.

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。
English: Huh.

Japanese: シッパイみたいだよ。 ‥‥ジッケン‥‥。
English: Well, I'm afraid your test results are negative.

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は、はいッ！
English: Y-yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: 早くしてください！
English: Hurry up with it!

Japanese: こういうのは、テンポが大事です！
English: We have a certain tempo we have to maintain here!
Yes... yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: では、今度こそ お見せしましょう‥‥。
English: Right. Here goes!

Japanese: 証拠は、これです！
English: This is my proof!

Japanese: ‥‥これは‥‥ さっきの写真、ですか‥‥？
English: This is... the photo from before?

Japanese: これが、今回の事件の“動機” を物語っているのです！
English: This photo reveals the motive in this murder!

Japanese: そうですね？　姫神さん！
English: I'm right, aren't I, Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: う‥‥ううう‥‥！
English: Hrrrrrrm... hmph!

Japanese: ５年前、スターダムの頂点に 立っていたイブクロさんは、
English: Five years ago, Mr. Hammer was at the height of his fame.

Japanese: 姫神　サクラのチカラで、 事故をもみ消してもらった。
English: With Dee Vasquez's help, a terrible accident was swept under the carpet...

Japanese: ‥‥今回の事件は、
English: Ladies and gentlemen, the case currently up for trial...

Japanese: そのときから、 始まっていたんです。
English: It began on that day, five years ago!

Japanese: ヒニクなことに、イブクロさんは この事故をキッカケに、
English: Ironically enough, that accident precipitated Mr. Hammer's fall...

Japanese: スターの座から転落することに なってしまいました。
English: His fall from stardom!

Japanese: ‥‥ツミの意識が あったのだろうな‥‥。
English: His guilt weighed him down, no doubt.

Japanese: しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: 姫神　サクラは、そんな イブクロさんを利用した。
English: You, Dee Vasquez, used Mr. Hammer.

Japanese: 格安のギャラで、イブクロさんを さんざんコキ使ったのです。
English: You made him work for you for petty change!

Japanese: ‥‥フン。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: では、今回のアクダイカーン の役も‥‥？
English: Bringing us to the present day... and Mr. Hammer's last role as the Evil Magistrate!

Japanese: イブクロさんにしてみれば、 クツジョクだったでしょうね。
English: Yes! Undoubtedly, it was a constant source of shame for the ex-star...

Japanese: うむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください、 成歩堂くん。
English: One moment, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥さっきからあなたは、 イブクロさんの話ばかりしている。
English: We are talking about motives, here... yet you have only talked about Mr. Hammer.

Japanese: これではまるで‥‥、
English: It almost seems as if...

Japanese: イブクロさんが、姫神　サクラを 殺害したように聞こえます。
English: As if it was Mr. Hammer who had killed Dee Vasquez, not the other way around!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 姫神さんの方はどうなんですか？
English: What motive would Ms. Vasquez have?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうさ。
English: Y-yes, that's right.

Japanese: あんたの話によれば、
English: According to what you say...

Japanese: アタクシにとって、イブクロは いい金ヅルじゃないか。
English: I would have had no reason to kill Hammer. He was a good source of income for me!

Japanese: そんなツゴウのいいオトコ、 アタクシは手ばなしたくないね！
English: And I never get rid of useful men. It's a policy of mine.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 彼女の動機を教えてください。
English: Please explain Ms. Vasquez's motive for murder.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Okay...

Japanese: では、姫神さんの動機を示す 証拠品をお見せしましょう。
English: Allow me to present to the court evidence proving Ms. Vasquez's motive!

Japanese: その証拠は、これですよ！
English: This is my proof!

Japanese: それが、姫神さんの動機を物語る 証拠品ですか？
English: And... this tells us why Ms. Vasquez wanted to kill Mr. Hammer?

Japanese: 弁護士の狂気を物語る証拠品 じゃない？
English: I'd say all it proves is that the defense attorney has completely lost his marbles.

Japanese: （うまいコト言いやがって‥‥）
English: (Urk!)

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥シッカリしてください！
English: Mr. Wright! The court requests that you find your marbles immediately.

Japanese: これは、ペナルティを 受けていただきます！
English: And, I'm afraid I have to penalize your for this waste of the court's time.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥あとイッポなんだよぉ！
English: Nick! We're so close!

Japanese: おねがいだから、よく考えてよぉ！
English: Think this one through, please!

Japanese: 弁護人。 ‥‥もう一度聞きますよ。
English: Mr. Wright, I'll ask you again.

Japanese: 彼女に、動機なんかありません。
English: She had no motive, Your Honor.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: What's that now!?

Japanese: 彼女はただ、階段からつきおとした だけなんです。
English: It was Mr. Hammer who was out for blood!

Japanese: 殺意をもっておそいかかってきた イブクロさんを、ね！
English: All Ms. Vasquez did was push him off the stairs... in self defense!

Japanese: な、なんだと！
English: What's that now!?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: It's simple!

Japanese: 殺意があったのは、 イブクロさんの方だったんですよ！
English: Mr. Hammer was intending to kill Ms. Vasquez!
Wha-!?

Japanese: なんだってェ！！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、睡眠薬で 荷星さんを眠らせた！
English: Jack Hammer drugged Mr. Powers with sleeping pills!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、楽屋にしのびこみ、 着ぐるみを盗んだ！
English: Jack Hammer snuck into the dressing room and stole the Steel Samurai costume!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、それを着て オバチャンの目をごまかした！
English: Jack Hammer wore the costume to fool the security lady into thinking he was Powers!

Japanese: そして衣袋　武志は、 コテージへ向かった！
English: Then Jack Hammer made his way to the trailer!

Japanese: いったい、なんのために！
English: And for what purpose, you ask?

Japanese: 自分をさんざん利用した、 姫神　サクラを殺害するためです！
English: To kill Dee Vasquez, who had so cruelly taken advantage of him all those years!

Japanese: あなたがやったんですね？
English: So, you did do it, didn't you?
... Yes.

Japanese: 犯人は、このアタクシよ。
English: I am guilty.

Japanese: このアタクシが‥‥。
English: It was me...

Japanese: “イシャだ！　早くしろ！”
English: "Someone call an ambulance!"

Japanese: “‥‥す、すごい血だ‥‥。 もう、手おくれじゃあ‥‥？”
English: "Th-there's blood everywhere! We're too late!"

Japanese: “あっ！　ひ、姫神さん！”
English: "Aah! Ms. Vasquez!"

Japanese: “これはいったい‥‥？ どうしたの！　何があったの！”
English: "What...? What happened here!?"

Japanese: “‥‥タクミさんが、コテージの 階段から落ちて‥‥”
English: "Manuel fell from the trailer stairs...!"

Japanese: “タ‥‥‥‥タクミくんが！”
English: "Manuel! Manuel!"

Japanese: “姫神さん！　おちついて！”
English: "Ms. Vasquez, please, try to remain calm..."

Japanese: “ウ‥‥ウソよ！ い、いったいどうして‥‥”
English: "No...! No! How could this...?"

Japanese: “事故だったんですよ”
English: "It was an accident..."

Japanese: “イブクロさんの アクションシーンで‥‥”
English: "Mr. Hammer was doing an action scene and..."

Japanese: “タクミくん！　タクミくーん！”
English: "No! Manuel! Manuel!!!"

Japanese: “さ、さわっちゃダメです！ 姫神さん！”
English: "D-don't touch him! Ms. Vasquez!"

Japanese: “いやああああああああああっ！”
English: "Nooooooooooooo!"

Japanese: “死なないで‥‥　おねがい‥‥”
English: "Please... please don't die."

Japanese: “おねがい‥‥”
English: "Please..."

Japanese: おめでとう。成歩堂くん。
English: Congratulations, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシの、負けね。
English: I lose... again.

Japanese: 御剣検事。 ‥‥姫神　サクラは‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth... Where's Dee Vasquez?

Japanese: 控え室に‥‥。 ‥‥冷静なものだ‥‥。
English: In the waiting lobby, Your Honor. As calm and collected as ever...

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。
English: I see...

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらまた、奇跡が 起こったようですね。
English: It appears you have brought about yet another miracle.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: I... thank you, Your Honor.

Japanese: それは違う、裁判長。
English: I think not, Your Honor.

Japanese: 荷星　三郎は、無実だった。
English: Will Powers was innocent.

Japanese: だから、それが証明された。 ‥‥あたりまえのことだ。
English: That he should be found so is only natural... not a miracle.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 そのとおりです。
English: Yes. Yes, you're right.

Japanese: では。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 荷星　三郎に対する判決を 言い渡しましょう。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Will Powers...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １０月２０日　午後１時１２分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: October 20, 1:12 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: もう、なんと言えばいいのか‥‥。
English: I... I don't know what to say!

Japanese: ホント、おせわになりました！
English: Thank you so much!

Japanese: よかったですね。
English: I'm just glad you're okay.

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥ただ‥‥。
English: Yes... but... it's sad.

Japanese: イブクロさん。ボクの 着ぐるみを盗んだってコトは、
English: I know now that Mr. Hammer stole my costume.

Japanese: 姫神プロデューサーを殺害して、 そのツミを、
English: It's bad enough that he wanted to kill Ms. Vasquez...

Japanese: 着ぐるみごと、ボクに着せようと したんでしょうね‥‥。
English: But to think that he tried to frame me for the murder!

Japanese: それだけが‥‥カナシイです。
English: It makes me sad...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　どうして！
English: Tell me why, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい？
English: H-huh?

Japanese: 教えてください！
English: Tell me!

Japanese: どうして、イブクロさんは、 ボクにツミを‥‥？
English: Why did Mr. Hammer want to blame me...?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 ぼくの考えを言いましょう。
English: Well, this is what I think.

Japanese: イブクロさんがあなたに ツミを着せようとしたのは‥‥
English: Mr. Hammer wanted to put the blame on you because...

Japanese: え？　ど、どういうことですか？
English: S-sorry? What does that mean?

Japanese: どういうことかな？　真宵ちゃん。
English: Yeah, what does that mean, Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Um, Nick? You know that problem of yours?

Japanese: また、やっちゃったね。
English: That problem where you present evidence that makes no sense? You're doing it again.

Japanese: それはあなたが“トノサマン” だからですよ。
English: It's because you were the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Because I was the Steel Samurai?

Japanese: イブクロさんは、かつては 大スターだった。
English: Mr. Hammer was a big star, once.

Japanese: なのに今は、子供番組の、 しかも悪役です。
English: But he was reduced to acting in a kids' show... and as the villain, to boot!

Japanese: 子供たちは、トノサマンが 大好きです。
English: The kids love the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: だから、彼はあなたを ニクんでしまったんでしょう。
English: And so, he hated you.

Japanese: や‥‥やっぱり、ですか‥‥。
English: I... I think I understand.

Japanese: ヒトコト、言ってくれれば。
English: H-he could have just told me.

Japanese: いつでも、かわって あげましたのに‥‥。
English: I would have changed places with him any time!

Japanese: （そういう問題じゃ ないと思うけど‥‥）
English: (I think you're missing the point...)

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: これで、この事件とも キッチリお別れできそうです。
English: I'm just glad it's all over.

Japanese: （‥‥よかったな‥‥）
English: (So am I... so am I...)

Japanese: ‥‥それは、今となっては 知りようもないですね。
English: I guess we'll never know.

Japanese: そ、そうですよね‥‥。
English: Y-yes, of course.

Japanese: （ふう‥‥。なんとか ごまかしたかな‥‥）
English: (Whew... I'm not sure I wanted to go down that road.)

Japanese: おめでとうございます！ ニボサブさん！
English: Congratulations, WP!

Japanese: いやっ、ははは。
English: Oh? Oh! Heh heh.

Japanese: みなさんのおかげで、 トノサマンをつづけられます！
English: Thanks to you, I'll be able to don the Steel Samurai outfit once more!

Japanese: うう‥‥早くあの汗くさい 着ぐるみを‥‥。
English: I can't wait to get back into that sweaty costume and...

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: Is... something wrong?

Japanese: い、いいえ！ なんでも‥‥
English: N-no! O-of course not...

Japanese: あっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ ‥‥御剣検事が‥‥！
English: N-Nick! Edgeworth!

Japanese: 成歩堂。‥‥なにか言え。
English: Say something, Wright.

Japanese: ニガテなのだ‥‥セケン話。
English: I'm not good at small talk.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: あ、ああ。
English: What?

Japanese: ザンネンだったな！　御剣！
English: Umm... that was too bad, Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥い、いきなりそれか。
English: You don't waste any time gloating, do you?

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとう。
English: No... I really want to thank you.

Japanese: きみの協力がなければ、 姫神に逃げられるところだったよ。
English: Vasquez would have gotten away if you hadn't stepped in.

Japanese: あ‥‥恐縮です。 ‥‥ニボシと申します。
English: Ah, uh, pleased to meet you. I'm Powers. *sniff*

Japanese: ‥‥む。ミツルギと申します。
English: Ah, er, Edgeworth.

Japanese: いつも、テレビでごカツヤクを ハイケンしております。
English: I'm a big fan of your work, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (Liar!)

Japanese: 成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: 私たちは、こうしてふたたび 出会ってしまった。
English: I must say, I hadn't expected to meet you again after all these years.
Meet "again"...?

Japanese: しかし。
English: However.

Japanese: 出会うべきではなかった。
English: In retrospect, it would have been better had we not met.

Japanese: おかげで私の中に、よけいな 感情がよみがえった。
English: Thanks to you, I am saddled with unnecessary... feelings.

Japanese: よけいな感情‥‥？
English: Unnecessary feelings?

Japanese: “不安”‥‥そして“迷い”だ。
English: Yes. Unease... and uncertainty.

Japanese: それは、よけいな感情じゃ ないだろう？
English: Aren't those kind of necessary?

Japanese: 私にとってはジャマなものだ。
English: They only serve to get in my way.

Japanese: いいか。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You listen to me, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: もう二度と、私の目の前に 現れないでほしい。
English: Don't ever show your face in front of me again.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけ、言いに来た。
English: That's what I came here to tell you.

Japanese: あのぉ、成歩堂さん？
English: Umm... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今のカタ、おトモダチなんですか？
English: Is... this guy your friend?

Japanese: そんな！　トモダチなわけ、 ないじゃないですか！
English: What? Friends? As if!

Japanese: テキですよ、テキ！ ‥‥ねっ！　なるほどくん！
English: They're rivals! Rivals! Right, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥今はね。
English: For now we are... I guess.

Japanese: ええーっ！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: なんでそう、思わせぶりなこと 言うかなぁ。
English: Why do I get the feeling I'm missing something?

Japanese: 教えてよー！
English: C'mon, Nick! Tell me! What's the deal with you and Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥こうして、この事件は、 ついに幕を下ろした。
English: And so the curtain closes on another trial...

Japanese: ぼくが法廷でぶちまけた イブクロさんの“事故”は、
English: I caused quite a stir by revealing that accident from five years ago.

Japanese: 大きく世間をさわがせることに なった。
English: It was the talk of the town.

Japanese: しかしその結果、英都撮影所は ふたたび方針を変えて、
English: Thankfully, Global Studios rethought its programming change.

Japanese: 子供のためのシリーズは、 つづけられることになった‥‥。
English: They went back to making kids' shows again.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 今日だよ、新番組！ ‥‥いっしょに見るんだからね！
English: This is it! The new show starts today! You're going to watch it with me, right?

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
English: (I'll admit, I was kind of surprised...)

Japanese: （ホンキでやるとは 思わなかったよ‥‥）
English: (I didn't think they'd seriously go through with it...)

Japanese: なるほどくんも買うんだよ！ トレーディング・カード。
English: You have to buy trading cards too, okay!

Japanese: 九太くんやスタッフの子と トレードするんだから！
English: We have to trade with Cody and that assistant!

Japanese: はいはい。 ‥‥わかったよ。
English: Fine, fine. I'll do it.

Japanese: でも、よかったね。 ニボサブさん、また主役だって！
English: Isn't it great that WP gets to play the lead again?

Japanese: 今度こそ、カオ、出るかなあ？
English: I wonder if they'll show his real face this time...
I dunno...

Japanese: ‥‥ますます出ないと思うよ。
English: I don't think the world is ready for the real Will Powers...

Japanese: おわり
English: THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: （し、しまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: 被告人、荷星　三郎に 判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Will Powers...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
